title: Vercel 确认遭入侵：第三方 AI 工具成突破口，黑客索要 200 万美元赎金
content: Vercel confirmed a security breach that began when attackers used a third‑party AI tool, Context.ai, to gain initial access via a compromised Google Workspace OAuth app. The breach exploited a “non‑sensitive” environment variable policy, allowing attackers to elevate privileges and access internal systems. A hacker claiming to be from “ShinyHunters” offered stolen data on the dark web and demanded $2 million in ransom. Vercel’s core services remain secure; the company is investigating and advising users to review and protect their environment variables.

title: 106 行业短信签名申请新规范出台， 5 月 1 日起生效
content: A new SMS signature regulation takes effect on May 1, 2026, requiring all signatures to be authentic, specific, and unique. Signatures must clearly identify the entity—short names cannot be vague, trademarks need accompanying company information unless uniquely registered, and ICP-registered website or app names are prohibited. Product-based signatures must include the parent company name. If a group uses a shared signature, a stamped authorization from the parent or trademark holder is required.

title: Focused microwaves allow 3D printers to fuse circuits onto almost anything
content: Microwave‑frequency heating can be used in additive manufacturing to fuse conductive inks directly onto virtually any surface, enabling 3D printers to embed functional electronic circuits into objects. This method leverages focused microwaves to locally heat and bond the metal particles in the ink, creating reliable connections without damaging the host material, thus widening the possibilities for embedding electronics in diverse, non‑conventional substrates.

title: 谋生型工作和兴趣型工作
content: The article reflects on how a person’s early focus—either on survival (谋生型工作) or on interest (兴趣型工作)—shapes their career trajectory. It argues that a high starting point is not measured by wealth but by having the security to pursue interests early. Those who grow up without safety concerns learn to defend themselves rather than explore, whereas those with security can engage in passion‑driven work that fuels long‑term growth. The author emphasizes that true success stems from early alignment of interest and career, not just financial gain.
source: 热帖 - 雪球 - 李药师2003

title: AI 就像那渣男渣女，平时哄着你温柔小意，关键时刻就坑你一波大的
content: Using AI heavily can create a dependence that makes people lazy and rely on it for most answers. Over time, this reliance can be exploited because AI may produce confident but erroneous or hallucinated responses that are hard to spot. Minor issues may appear harmless, but major problems can arise unnoticed. Users should remain skeptical, verify information, and keep control of critical thinking rather than delegating it to AI.

title: 部署本地模型 token 输出万能公式
content: This note explains that when deploying a 31 B GEMMA model in BF16 on a GPU with 273 GB/s memory bandwidth, each token takes about 227 ms, yielding a theoretical 4.4 tokens/s. Realistic performance is ~3 tokens/s, max 2.5. Key factors: the model must fit in GPU memory and the memory bandwidth must be sufficient; otherwise performance drops. Mac GPUs behave differently, allowing slower but usable rates (~20 tokens/s). Local deployment is mainly for experimentation; expect lower speed than online models, especially for complex code generation. Proper cooling is essential.

title: 前端重生之 - 前端视角下的 Python
content: The article reflects on the evolution of front‑end JavaScript and the author’s transition to Python. It compares event loops, concurrency models, GIL, functional vs. multi‑paradigm styles, and type systems (TypeScript vs. Python’s gradual typing). It contrasts package ecosystems (npm vs. PyPI), virtual environments, and the rich Python standard library. The piece highlights Python’s dominance in data science, the rise of browser‑side ML libraries, and the philosophical similarities between Django and Express. Finally it argues that language choice should be driven by problem context rather than allegiance.

title: 智谱 coding plan 的使用 token 限制是多少？
content: The user acquired the Lite coding plan from Zhipu last December and had not used it much. In April, Zhipu enabled GLM‑5.1 for Lite users and the user began frequent use, which led to two access blocks—first a temporary block that was lifted after reducing usage, and then a 30‑day block today. The user suspects the blocks are due to high token consumption. They ask for clarity on token limits for each coding plan, as Zhipu only publishes request‑rate limits, not token caps. title: 大家用 AI 是订阅制多还是 API 按量付费多？
content: Users primarily use AI through subscription models, but many also rely on API pay‑per‑use. When both are employed, the distribution usually depends on project requirements: subscriptions cover general usage, while APIs handle peak demands or specialized tasks. Cost comparisons show that subscriptions offer predictable pricing for steady workloads, whereas pay‑per‑use can be cheaper for sporadic or highly variable needs. In practice, the optimal mix is determined by balancing budget constraints and usage patterns.

title: Claude 45%-55%折渠道
content: Japanese AWS and Anthropic offer a multi‑layer discount structure. First, AWS Enterprise Discount Programs (EDP) give 5–15 % off on a $1 M minimum, scaling to ~25 % at $20 M. Second, Anthropic resellers add 5–10 % gross margin, but after fees only ~2 % remains. Third, domestic dealers (Classmethod, Server Works, etc.) provide flat discounts. Fourth, government subsidies can cover up to ¥45 M for cloud services, with AI‑specific grants up to ¥30 M. Fifth, AWS startup grants up to $300 k via Activate portfolios and GenAI Scale credits. Aggregating these, a Japanese entity can reduce Claude API costs to 45–55 % of the standard price, while maintaining physical isolation and data security.

title: Meta“Token榜”逼疯打工人，一夜烧掉公司几万刀，AI时代Token焦虑越来越离谱
content: Meta’s internal “Token榜” tracks employees’ AI token usage, with over 60 trillion tokens spent in a month—roughly an estimated $900 million. The ranking has become a status symbol, reflecting a Silicon Valley culture where higher token consumption equates to higher productivity and AI proficiency. Critics argue this metric conflates input with output, incentivizes wasteful token use, and overlooks the hidden costs of internal agent reasoning that inflate token counts without proportional value. The debate highlights a shift toward optimizing token efficiency rather than merely increasing usage.

content: I built a game demo quickly with AI assistance, then started refining the core. The AI‑generated code feels over‑structured yet behaves oddly, and since I didn’t write it, I’m reluctant to examine it. I lack a clear overall design, the AI’s naming clashes with my style, and I’m unfamiliar with the language’s framework. The takeaway is that developers must understand the architecture when using AI to code; otherwise, the generated code cannot be effectively optimized.

title: 人为什么总是会不自觉地逐渐滑向平庸？
content: People tend to drift into mediocrity without conscious effort, gradually settling into comfortable routines and familiar patterns that discourage innovation or risk-taking. Social pressures, fear of failure, and the desire for stability can reinforce complacency, leading individuals to prioritize immediate comfort over long‑term growth. As a result, their potential is underutilized, and they miss opportunities to challenge themselves and achieve higher levels of accomplishment.

title: 国产大模型集体转身
content: In the last 60 days, China’s top AI model firms pivoted from selling compute to selling tokens and subscription services. Domestic C‑end markets are saturated, and funding gaps are tightening, while overseas success stories—Anthropic’s token‑priced models and Cursor’s subscription‑based IDE plugin—show viable revenue models. However, China lacks a paid‑tool culture, mature B2B procurement systems, and high‑margin token pricing. Consequently, domestic firms face high costs and tight margins, making token‑economies difficult to replicate.

title: 第一代网红奶茶奈雪，怎么悄悄亏麻了?
content: Naïxü has become the only listed new‑tea brand in 2025 to post a loss, with revenue falling 12% and a ¥240 million loss. Its flagship “tea + soft‑bagel” model eroded when it cut in‑store freshly baked items for cost‑saving, replacing them with cold‑ware deliveries. Efforts to revive the fresh‑baked line came too late; bagel revenue dropped to 8.1% of total sales. Coupled with high rents and a focus on sit‑down “third‑space” venues, Naïxü’s high‑end positioning failed to attract a fast‑paced consumer base, leaving it lagging behind cheaper rivals.

title: 杨立昆开喷 Anthropic CEO：别信那个卖AI的人
content: Andrew LeCun criticized Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei for claiming AI will eliminate 50% of tech and white‑collar jobs in 1‑5 years. LeCun, former Meta researcher, counters by citing economic studies that show technology historically creates new roles, not just destroys them. He urges listeners to heed established labor‑economy scholars over AI executives, arguing that job impact is an economic, not technical, issue. The debate highlights broader concerns about AI’s real‑world workforce effects.

AI‑driven PCB demand will explode, reaching a projected 160 billion CNY market in 2027, driven by Nvidia’s Rubin platform, Google TPU, AWS Trainium, and high‑speed optical modules. Upstream materials—electronic glass fabric, copper foil, and resins—are inflating; price increases are passed down to CCL and PCB makers. Major Chinese suppliers (e.g., Zhuyuan, Shencun, Guotong) are expanding capacity, while firms such as Shenghong, Huidian, and Shennan are raising output and capital to meet rising orders. The industry faces supply‑chain inflation, TSMC packaging delays, but core AI server demand keeps growth robust.

title: 江同志为啥不焦虑 | 原文
content: In July 1947, amid war and inflation, Jiang was about to graduate and face daunting job prospects. Jiang’s focus on collective action and resistance helped him avoid anxiety about uncertain employment prospects.

title: Vercel Says Internal Systems Hit in Breach
content: Vercel disclosed that an internal systems breach was detected. The company confirmed that unauthorized access impacted only internal infrastructure and they are investigating the incident. No external data, customer information, or public-facing services were reported as compromised. Vercel remains committed to safeguarding user data and is reviewing its security controls following the breach.

title: Notion leaks email addresses of all editors of any public page
content: Notion has inadvertently exposed the email addresses of every editor who has contributed to any public page. The breach affects all users who have edited such pages, potentially allowing unauthorized access or spam. The incident highlights concerns about data privacy and platform security. Users are advised to review their account settings and consider changing passwords to mitigate risks.

title: NASA 关闭旅行者 1 号的 LECP 仪器以节省电力维持运行
content: NASA’s JPL engineers have commanded the Voyager 1 probe to shut down its Low Energy Charged Particle (LECP) instrument to conserve power, as the spacecraft’s radioisotope thermoelectric generator is losing about 4 W per year. LECP, which provided crucial data on the interstellar medium, has been one of seven instruments deactivated after 49 years. The shutdown is expected to give the probe roughly one year of spare power, during which engineers will refine a “Big Bang” energy‑saving plan to extend its remaining mission life.
